content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

March 16, 1977

Robert Hogeboom
Barger & Wolen
333 S. Hope Street, Thirty-eighth floor
Los Angeles, California 90071

Dear Mr. Hogeboom:

This is to confirm our telephone conversation today in
which I provided you with advice concerning Richards E.
Barger, a registered lobbyist who is a partner in your law
firm.

Mr. Barger, a registered lobbyist for two life insurance companies, plans to lobby a bill which would affect
several other life insurance companies and some life insurance associations. None of these other companies nor the
associations will be paying Mr. Barger a fee nor do any of
these entities retain the law firm for any purpose whatsoever.

I advised you today that Mr. Barger must report the
specific bill on his lobbyist and lobbyist employer reports.
However, none of the life insurance companies or associations
which do not compensate Mr. Barger need file lobbyist employer reports since the Political Reform Act in Government Code
§86108 provides that only persons who employ or contract for
the services of a lobbyist must file lobbyist employer reports.

Please feel free to call me if you have any further
questions.

Very truly yours,

Robert M. Stern
General Counsel

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6641

LAW OFFICES

BARGER & WOLEN
333 SOUTH HOPE STREET, THIRTY-EIGHTH FLOOR
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90071
TELEPHONE (213) 660-2800
CABLE ADDRESS: DARWOL

RICHARDS D. BARGER
ALAN D. WOLEN
WILLIAM D. HARRISON
KENT KELLER
DARLEY C. FROST
THOMAS S. ACKLAND
HARRY S. STAHL
ROBERT W. HOGEBOOM
STEPHEN C. KLEIN
RUSSELL M. FRANGSEN
S. STUART SOLDATE
D. STEPHEN BRITT

NEWPORT BEACH OFFICE
4249 MACARTHUR BLVD
SECOND FLOOR
NEWPORT BEACH, CA 92660
TELEPHONE (714) 767-5400

SAN FRANCISCO OFFICE
555 CALIFORNIA STREET
EIGHTEENTH FLOOR
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94104
*TELEPHONE (415) 562-4900

PLEASE REFER TO
OUR FILE NUMBER:

811-00

February 22, 1977

Mr. Rich Eichman
Fair Political Practice Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Lobbyist Reports

Dear Rich:

     Would you please provide some insight into the
question concerning filing requirements for lobbyists who
are not compensated for their lobbying activity. Richards
D. Barger, a registered lobbyist desires to lobby the
California legislature concerning insurance regulations
for which he is not being compensated. Such lobbying will
benefit certain life insurance companies. Please keep in
mind that Mr. Barger has undertaken such lobbying without
remuneration and as such is not "employed" as a lobbyist.
My questions are two fold:

     1) Whether Mr. Barger is required to register
        the above-mentioned activity as lobbyist
        activity.

     2) Whether each of the life insurance companies
        on whose behalf Mr. Barger is lobbying but
        receiving no remuneration must file periodic
        reports.

     I have noted that Section 86108 of the Government
Code states that "Any person who employs or contracts for the
services of one or more lobbyists,..." is required to file

LAW OFFICES
BARGER & WOLEN

Mr. Rich Eichman
February 22, 1977
Page Two


periodic reports. In our case, no person or entity has
employed or contracted for the services of Mr. Barger.

Thank you for your courtesy and cooperation in
this matter.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
ROBERT W. HOGEBOOM

sections.conclusion: Mr. Berger must report the specific bill on his lobbyist and lobbyist employer reports. However, none of the life insurance companies or associations which do not compensate Mr. Berger need file lobbyist employer reports since the Political Reform Act in Government Code 56108 provides that only persons who employ or contract for the services of a lobbyist must file lobbyist employer reports.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a registered lobbyist report lobbying activity for which he receives no compensation, and must the uncompensated entities file lobbyist employer reports under Government Code Section 56108?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The registered lobbyist must report the lobbying activity on his lobbyist reports, but entities that do not compensate him are not required to file lobbyist employer reports because Government Code Section 56108 requires reporting only by those who employ or contract for lobbyist services.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request (February 22, 1977 from Barger & Wolen) and FPPC's response (March 1977 from Robert A. Stern). Minor OCR artifacts present but content is clearly legible. Telephonic advice was confirmed in writing.

CONCLUSION: Mr. Berger must report the specific bill on his lobbyist and lobbyist employer reports. However, none of the life insurance companies or associations which do not compensate Mr. Berger need file lobbyist employer reports since the Political Reform Act in Government Code 56108 provides that only persons who employ or contract for the services of a lobbyist must file lobbyist employer reports.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises that a registered lobbyist must report uncompensated lobbying activity on his personal reports, but the entities benefiting from such lobbying are not required to file employer reports unless they actually compensate or contract for the services.
